Track Record — Financial Services Issuer Services

Describe your organization's overall track record serving card-issuing financial institutions. Include years in market, number of active issuer clients, approximate ACV managed, and any notable performance milestones.

Sutherland has been a dedicated partner to card-issuing financial institutions for over 19 years, serving debit, credit, and prepaid issuers across Tier-1 banks, specialized card issuers, NBFCs, and fintechs. 

Today, we support more than 25 active issuer clients globally including 3 of the top 5 card issuers globally representing approximately 70% of issuance volume across our portfolio. We are the industry leader in Cards Issuance and Payments space - owing to our domain depth, variety of clientele, scale & size, value and impact to clients through Digital transformation and balanced global delivery footprint. 
Our aggregate ACV across these Card issuer and Payment companies relationships exceeds $100 million. Cards & Payments is the single largest practice within our Financial services portfolio.
Some of our clients include leading Global Cards and Payment issuers, specialized prime and sub-prime card issuers, leading global Card network provider, Global fleet pre-paid cards provider, leading Remittance companies, mid sized and super regional banks, and more.

Our industry expertise and leadership in this space has been recognized by analysts: Everest Group named Sutherland a Leader in its Payments Business Process Services (BPS) PEAK Matrix® Assessment 2025 (this ranking is across Cards and Payments), evaluated on both market impact and vision and capability. Our ranking on this Everest report are in fact higher than FIS itself.

We have consistently grown, added new clients and expanded our offerings for Cards and Payment companies. Over the last 3 years, we have added 5 new clients in this space, launched two relevant offerings (Sutherland FRAML, Sutherland FinTelligent), which are geared to bring value and transformation to our clients.

Our performance track record across issuer engagements reflects measurable, sustained outcomes:

•	TCO reduction of >30% through process digital transformation, re-engineering, right-shoring, and hyper-automation leveraging Sutherland's proprietary IP (Robility, FinXelerate, Fintelligent, CX360, Agent Assist, HelpTree, and Sentinel)
•	Customer journey ideation and redesign at Sutherland LABS, helping create new ways to interact and interface with end clients.
•	98% KPI achievement consistently across active contracts
•	CXi (Customer Experience Index) improvement of >10% driven by our Design Thinking-based approach to process enhancement and omnichannel service delivery
•	Significant NPS uplift and measurable reduction in customer complaints, underpinned by real-time analytics and agent-assist tooling
•	Proven outcome-based and gainsharing commercial models, with co-investment commitments that align Sutherland's incentives directly with client value creation

Sutherland's Cards and Payments services spans the full issuing value chain from onboarding and underwriting through account servicing, transaction processing, disputes and chargebacks, and FCC operations; supported by a balanced offshore and nearshore footprint that enables flexibility, seasonal scalability, and regulatory proximity.

Describe your approach to managing sub-contractors used in delivering TMS services. Confirm: (1) whether sub-contractors would be used on this engagement, which subcontractors would be used and where work would be performed; (2) your SLA flow-down mechanism; (3) whether the client retains audit rights over sub-contractors.

Our approach to managing sub contractors for TMS services is governed by a standardized third party risk management framework covering due diligence, contracting, information security, regulatory compliance, and ongoing performance oversight. Our foundational principle is straightforward; Sutherland remains the prime contractor at all times, and full accountability for delivery performance, service quality, and contractual obligations rests with Sutherland and not with any sub-contractor.

1.	Use of sub contractors
For this engagement, we may plan to use multiple sub-contractors. For e.g., we plan to use Uniphore as a specialized sub contractor to provide the unified desktop and real time agent assist solution that underpins our digital transformation and CX optimization objectives. Uniphore’s platform will be used to deliver unified agent workflows and real time guidance from approved locations, while all core service delivery, governance, and client facing roles remain with Sutherland as the prime contractor. We are utilizing Uniphore as a sub-contractor to provide AI services to FIS already.
There maybe a need to evaluate possible use of additional sub-contractors to deliver services in EMEA region. We also constantly evaluate best in industry AI solutions to bring the best of global AI tools and technology to deliver the transformation solutions we are proposing as part of our response. Should any additional sub contractors be required as the engagement evolves, Sutherland will notify the client in advance, provide full details of scope, location, and controls, and obtain the client’s prior written approval before onboarding.

2.	SLA flow down mechanism
All SLAs, security, confidentiality, data protection, and regulatory obligations agreed with the client are contractually flowed down to our sub-contractors through back to back agreements that mirror the requirements in the prime contract. We operate a single integrated SLA and governance framework in which our sub-contractors performance is monitored alongside Sutherland’s operations via common dashboards and review forums, with defined remediation actions and the right to invoke service credits or adjust or terminate the sub contract arrangement in the event of persistent under performance. In summary, overall performance of this engagement (including anything sent to sub-contractor) will be the responsibility of Sutherland.

3.	Client audit rights over sub contractors
The client will retain full audit and inspection rights, as defined in the master services agreement, over material sub contractors used in delivering TMS services, including our sub-contractors. These rights (such as access to relevant records, processes, and control evidence, subject to reasonable notice and confidentiality) are embedded in our contract with our partner sub-contractors to ensure that the client’s and regulators’ audit and oversight requirements can be met seamlessly across the delivery chain.

How many years has your organization delivered end-to-end risk mitigation covering fraud, collections, disputes, and credit bureau management. Report the number of years your organization has continuously operated this function. Do not sum individual staff years across people — report the longest unbroken period your organization has actively delivered this service.

Sutherland has continuously delivered end-to-end risk mitigation services spanning fraud management, collections, disputes and chargebacks, and credit bureau management for over 15 years, with this fully integrated capability operational since 2010. This represents the longest unbroken period during which our organization has actively delivered all four functions as a cohesive service offering.
Our Risk Management practice (FinCrime) is over 2500 FTEs across 28+ clients in Card issuers, Payment providers, Banks, Fintechs, Lenders, global e-com giants, retailers, Travel and Airlines and more. 

We have been rated as Star Performer -Financial Crime and Compliance (FCC) Operations Services PEAK Matrix® Assessment 2025. This rating validates our growing industry dominance and leadership in the FinCrime space.

We provide end to end of FinCrime and Risk Management services which include Identity checks, KYC and Onboarding, Screening & Watchlists review, EDD investigations, Transaction Monitoring services, Alert remediations, Disputes & Chargebacks, Forensic audits, LOD 2,3 services, Risk Control and Testing.

In Dec 2025, we announced a partnership with a leading Global AML platform provider (Comply Advantage). We provide "Compliance as a service" as part of this managed services offering that cuts across the entire FinCrime value chain.

Over the last few years, we have developed numerous in-house AI solutions to help accelerate value for our clients. Some of these solutions include Disputes hyper-automation, KYC Onboarding tool, ID Scan and Fraud verification tool, PII data masking and compliance tool.

How many years has your organization delivered customer care for card programs (commercial, consumer credit, debit, retail, loyalty, healthcare, prepaid). Scope includes: IVR and live agent handling, multilingual and after-hours support, lost/stolen servicing, and loyalty programs. Report the number of years your organization has continuously operated this function. Do not sum individual staff years across people — report the longest unbroken period your organization has actively delivered this service.

Sutherland has continuously delivered end-to-end customer care for 37 years now, and for card programs for over 18 years, with unbroken operations since 2007. This encompasses the full scope of card servicing including IVR and live agent handling, multilingual and after-hours support, lost/stolen card servicing, and loyalty program management.

Our Customer services practice services over 50 clients today with 8,000 FTEs across various services and product lines. Our services span from Customer Insight and journey mapping to Onboarding and servicing operations, query resolutions across multiple channels (Voice, chat, email, social media), Retention, loyalty management. 
We provide Customer support across the Financial services portfolio, including multiple scenarios like Lost/stolen, Dispute resolution, Payment processing, loyalty management etc.

As part of our Customer services portfolio, we provide "Customer services of the Future", which brings together the best of CX technology and Agentic AI solutions. We offer Omni-channel support through our own platform "Connect"; Agent productivity enhancement AI tools (e.g. Agent assit, GenAI based Knowledgebase, Unified Desktop); Agentic Conversational AI to enhance end customer experience, and reporting and management tools.

We have delivered tremendous value to our clients in Customer services over the years including:
- 40% TCO reduction
- 15% CSAT Improvement
- 10% improvement in First call resolution
- 15% reduction in customer complaints

Sutherland is an industry leader in providing Customer service, especially to Financial institutions. Our experience, depth of domain, AI led Digital transformation solutions puts us in the best position to continuously bring value to our clients.

How many years has your organization delivered back-office processing for card programs. Scope includes: imaging, correspondence processing, payment research, balance transfers, credit balance refunds, account maintenance, general ledger (GL) balancing and settlement, reconciliation of GL accounts, and returned mail processing. Note: application processing is not in scope for this engagement. Report the number of years your organization has continuously operated this function. Do not sum individual staff years across people — report the longest unbroken period your organization has actively delivered this service.

Sutherland has continuously delivered back-office processing for card programs for over 15 years now. We deliver or have delivered all back office scope items as detailed in this RFP. 
Our back office services include: Onboarding / application processing, pre-screening checks, compliance and KYC validations, Screening and Monitoring, activations and funding, account servicing and maintenance, waivers, card adjustments, claims adjudication, account closures, payments and remmitance exceptions processing, chargebacks and RegE disputes processing, Fraud prevention and investigations, reconciliations and GL balancing and settlements.

We have over 2500 FTEs that deliver above back office functions across Cards, Payments, Retail banks, Lenders, Fintechs and BNPL providers.

We also have a mailrooom facility in Tulsa, US with limited capacity to handle checks, return mails, imaging, lockbox etc.
Years of Experience — Client Operations Oversight

How many years has your organization delivered structured client-facing operational support. Scope includes: day-to-day client inquiry and escalation management, monthly and quarterly business reviews, and SLA monitoring. Report the number of years your organization has continuously operated this function. Do not sum individual staff years across people — report the longest unbroken period your organization has actively delivered this service.

Sutherland has delivered structured client-facing operational support, including day-to-day client inquiry and escalation management, monthly and quarterly business reviews, and SLA monitoring, for a continuous period of Thiry Five (35)+ years.

Client facing operational support is embedded and delivered as part of every single operational services that we provide to our clients. We have done this for over 35 years, right from the time the company has started.
Over years, we have developed structures, acquired and implemented best in class industry certifications (like ISO, PCIDSS) and introduced numerous AI platforms and tools to deliver these functions effectively for our clients.
Number of Active Financial Services Clients
How many active financial services clients does your organization currently serve? Break out by client type (issuer, acquirer, payments processor, other) where possible.

Sutherland currently partners with over 85 active banking and financial services clients worldwide, spanning card issuers, Retail and Commercial banks, payments and fintech providers, Lenders, Fintechs, BNPL providers, Asset managers and broader financial institutions globally.
While we do not formally segment our active client count by issuer, acquirer, and processor in public disclosures, our portfolio includes clients across all three categories. Issuers and payments/fintech providers represent the majority of our active base, reflecting Sutherland's long-standing strategic focus on the card and payments ecosystem. Within the issuer segment specifically, Sutherland currently serves 3 of the top 5 card issuers globally, with coverage spanning consumer credit, debit, commercial, prepaid, and loyalty card programs.

We deliver Customer services and / or back offices services from most of our site globally today. 
A list of some sites where we deliver these include:

United States: Tulsa (OK), Rochester (NY), California, New Jersey, New York, Oklahoma, Virginia, and Work From Home across 45+ states in the US
Canada: Windsor

Egypt: Alexandria, Cairo

Sutherland operates over 60 operation centers and business operations in these countries. The majority of these sites are company-owned. Additionally, Sutherland has a robust Work From Home (WFH) model in several countries, including the United States, China, and Portugal. There is no indication in the context of sub-contracted sites; all referenced sites are either company-owned or operated directly by Sutherland.

Which languages do you currently support across your delivery network? List associated delivery locations. Of particular relevance: English (US, CA, UK), Spanish (US), French-Canadian, German, Dutch, Portuguese.

Sutherland currently supports over 40 languages across its global delivery network, with associated delivery locations as follows:

English: Supported in the United States (Tulsa, OK; Rochester, NY; WFH across 45+ states), Canada (Windsor), United Kingdom, Bulgaria (Sofia, Varna, Burgas), Kosovo (Pristina), Egypt (Alexandria, Cairo), China (Suzhou), Malaysia (Kuala Lumpur), Japan (Tokyo), Colombia (Bogota, Barranquilla), India (multiple cities), Philippines (multiple cities), Dubai (UAE), and other global centers.
Spanish: Supported in the United States, Colombia (Bogota, Barranquilla), Bulgaria, and Mexico.
French-Canadian: Supported in Canada (Windsor).
German: Supported in Bulgaria (Sofia, Varna, Burgas), Kosovo (Pristina), Egypt (Alexandria, Cairo), India (select locations), Dubai (UAE).
Dutch: Supported in Bulgaria (Sofia, Varna, Burgas).
Portuguese (including Brazilian Portuguese): Supported in Colombia (Bogota, Barranquilla), Bulgaria, and Brazil.

Additional languages supported include Arabic, Bahasa, Bulgarian, Burmese, Cantonese, Danish, Estonian, Finnish, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Malay, Mandarin, Norwegian, Polish, Russian, Swedish, Tagalog, Thai, Turkish, Vietnamese, and more. Sutherland leverages its sites in Eastern Europe, Asia Pacific, and Latin America as primary delivery locations for multiple languages in single locations, and bilingual support is available at several centers.

What is your total headcount across all geographies? Break out by: (1) front-office agent FTE; (2) back-office agent FTE; (3) support FTE (QA, WFM, Training, Management, Analytics); (4) approximate onshore / nearshore / offshore split (%).

Sutherland has a total headcount of over 55,000 full-time employees, with an additional 4,000–8,000 FTEs added during peak seasons. The breakdown by role function is as follows:

Front-office agent FTE: 51% spread across 25% Customer Service (Customer Care), 13% Technical Support Helpdesk Digital Engineering Services, 7% Collections, 6% Sales, Customer Acquisition, Retention
Back-office agent FTE: 31% spread across 16% Backoffice – Transaction Processing, 8% Finance & Accounting Services, 4% Content Moderation Management, 3% Research & Consulting
Support FTE (QA, WFM, Training, Management, Analytics): 5% Support (HR, Infosec, QA, WFM, etc.), 6% Operations Management, 5% HR Services, 2% Admin Exec, 2% Quality, 1% WFM, 0.7% HR Training, 3.0% Service Delivery Management & Support, 0.3% Analytics
The approximate onshore, nearshore, offshore split is:

Onshore: 13% (North America)
Nearshore: 17% (LATAM and Caribbean)
Offshore: 70% (EMEA and APAC)
What were your voluntary agent attrition rates for each of the last three calendar years? Break out by geography where materially different.

Sutherland's attrition rate globally is between 3-3.65% for the last three years. Our attrition rates are some of the best in the industry, specially in US, LATAM and India which typically tend to experience higher attrition rates in the industry.

Sutherland's global average voluntary attrition rate for front-line employees hired greater than 180 days is 3.65% per month, which equates to 44% annually for calendar year 2025. 

Attrition rates by geography for CY 2025 are as follows: Bulgaria 2.4%, Canada 3.8%, Colombia 4.6%, Egypt 4.9%, India 2.7%, Jamaica 4.4%, Kosovo 2.5%, Malaysia 2.4%, Mexico 2.6%, Philippines 3.9%, South Africa 3.2%, UAE 1.0%, and US 4.2%. Attrition for management and professional roles is significantly lower at 0.5% per month (6% annual).

What is your average agent tenure overall? Break out by primary function where available: Customer Service, Fraud Operations, Disputes & Chargebacks, Debt & Collections, Back Office, and Risk & Compliance.

Our current global average frontline agent tenure across financial services operations is approximately 2.3 years. By primary function, average tenure is: Customer Service 1.8 years; Fraud Operations 2.4 years; Disputes & Chargebacks 2.8 years; Debt & Collections 2.3 years; Back Office 3.5 years; and Risk & Compliance 3.8 years. 
These figures reflect actual time in role and are not based on summed staff experience
Optional. Are there any industry-relevant certifications, training frameworks, or accreditations held by your workforce that are applicable to financial services contact center operations?

Sutherland’s workforce holds a comprehensive suite of industry-relevant certifications, training frameworks, and accreditations applicable to financial services contact center operations. These include PCI, ISO 27001, ISO 27701, ISO 9001, ISO 14001, ISO 45001, P-CMM Level 5, Six Sigma, HIPAA, GDPR, EU-U.S. Privacy Shield Framework, and COPC. 

In additional, For Financial services industry, based on role and job requirement, we have resources certified as CFE, ACAMS, Internal Audits, ABA, NMLS, FCA amongst others. We either hire resources with these certifications or sponsor and certify our resources on these based on job requirements.

Sutherland also participates in the EcoVadis sustainability assessment and utilizes Trustwave’s TrustKeeper Compliance Validation Service for PCI DSS. 

The organization maintains a large in-house talent pool trained in COPC standards and Six Sigma methodologies, and operates under a Service Excellence Framework based on best practices. 

Additionally, Sutherland provides targeted ethics and compliance training, including anti-fraud, anti-money laundering, and anti-bribery policies, and supports ongoing regulatory financial compliance training for all employees. For financial services programs, 

Sutherland employs licensed mortgage loan officers and insurance representatives, ensuring all required state and federal licenses are obtained and maintained through structured training and continuing education. The organization also leverages resources such as Thomas Reuters for International Banking Regulations and BAI Learning Management for U.S. Banking and Financial Regulations as needed.

Sutherland maintains role-based training and governance frameworks relevant to financial services contact center operations, including information security, data protection, process excellence, and simulation based operational training. Where applicable, our teams also operate within regulated control environments aligned to card-payment and financial-services requirements

Vendor Response — How Your Solution Meets This Requirement
Disclose all sub-contractors by name, location, and scope. Confirm SLA flow-down and FIS audit rights over all 4th parties.

For this engagement, we may plan to use multiple sub-contractors. For e.g., we plan to use Uniphore as a specialized sub contractor to provide the unified desktop and real time agent assist solution that underpins our digital transformation and CX optimization objectives. Uniphore’s platform will be used to deliver unified agent workflows and real time guidance from approved locations, while all core service delivery, governance, and client facing roles remain with Sutherland as the prime contractor. We are utilizing Uniphore as a sub-contractor to provide AI services to FIS already.
There maybe a need to evaluate possible use of additional sub-contractors to deliver services in EMEA region. We also constantly evaluate best in industry AI solutions to bring the best of global AI tools and technology to deliver the transformation solutions we are proposing as part of our response. Should any additional sub contractors be required as the engagement evolves, Sutherland will notify the client in advance, provide full details of scope, location, and controls, and obtain the client’s prior written approval before onboarding.

2.	SLA flow down mechanism
All SLAs, security, confidentiality, data protection, and regulatory obligations agreed with the client are contractually flowed down to our sub-contractors through back to back agreements that mirror the requirements in the prime contract. We operate a single integrated SLA and governance framework in which our sub-contractors performance is monitored alongside Sutherland’s operations via common dashboards and review forums, with defined remediation actions and the right to invoke service credits or adjust or terminate the sub contract arrangement in the event of persistent under performance. In summary, overall performance of this engagement (including anything sent to sub-contractor) will be the responsibility of Sutherland.

3.	Client audit rights over sub contractors
The client will retain full audit and inspection rights, as defined in the master services agreement, over material sub contractors used in delivering TMS services, including our sub-contractors. These rights (such as access to relevant records, processes, and control evidence, subject to reasonable notice and confidentiality) are embedded in our contract with our partner sub-contractors to ensure that the client’s and regulators’ audit and oversight requirements can be met seamlessly across the delivery chain.
Describe your internal governance model for monitoring sub-contractor performance and compliance.

Sutherland uses a standardized third‑party risk and vendor governance framework to oversee all sub‑contractors supporting our services. Sub‑contractors are risk‑tiered, with clear ownership, SLAs/KPIs aligned to client commitments, and regular performance reviews and scorecards. Compliance is monitored through periodic risk assessments, security and data‑protection checks, incident reporting and remediation, and participation in internal and client/regulatory audits, with contractual remedies available where performance or compliance falls below agreed thresholds.
Confirm cardholder data will not leave approved jurisdictions without explicit client consent. Describe data residency controls.

To confirm, cardholder data (CHD) is strictly confined to approved jurisdictions through a combination of geographic pinning and rigorous technical egress filters, ensuring no data crosses borders without explicit client consent. This is enforced by hosting sensitive workloads in regional "silos"—physically isolated data centers—where infrastructure-as-code policies block the provisioning of storage in unauthorized territories. To prevent accidental or malicious transfers, Data Loss Prevention (DLP) tools and geofencing are deployed to monitor and intercept outbound traffic containing Primary Account Numbers (PANs). These residency controls are validated through continuous automated data discovery and annual audits to maintain compliance with both regional legal mandates and PCI DSS standards
Describe the regulatory notification process before offshoring commences (e.g. FCA Principal 11 notification — UK; WARN Act — US). Confirm who is responsible for filings.

Sutherland would be responsible for regulatory notification process (provided we have completed rebadging and site take over processes).

Regulated firms in the UK must adhere to strict notification requirements under the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (FSMA), FCA Principles for Businesses (Principle 11), and Prudential Regulation Authority (PRA) rules. 
Process:
Materiality Assessment: Before notifying, firms must determine if the offshoring arrangement is "material" (i.e., its failure would cause intolerable harm to clients, the firm, or the stability of the UK financial system).
Advance Notification: Firms must give advance notice to the FCA or PRA of any proposal to enter into a material outsourcing arrangement or significant changes to existing arrangements.
Principle 11 Notification: Deals with regulators "in an open and cooperative way" and includes disclosing anything the regulator would reasonably expect notice of.
Responsible Party: The governing body and senior management of the authorized UK firm.

The federal Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification (WARN) Act requires employers to provide notice before massive layoffs or plant closures caused by moving operations offshore. 
Process:
Notification Requirements: Covered employers must provide 60 calendar days' advance written notice of a plant closing or mass layoff to employees (or their representatives/unions), the state's rapid-response dislocated worker unit, and the chief elected official of the local government.
Triggering Events: A plant closing affecting 50 or more employees at a single site, or a mass layoff involving 500+ employees, or 50–499 employees if they make up 33% of the workforce.
Responsible Party: The employer (parent company or subsidiary), generally determined by the entity managing the facility and employment relationships.
Describe your approach to clients with explicit offshore restrictions in their current TMS contracts.

There are several benefits of offshoring and nearshoring for clients in terms of cost, productivity and improcing digitization of processes, resulting in a better end-customer experience.

Sutherland would bring in a team of experts in regulatory compliance, legal, global operations leader, BFS leadership, transition &  transformation and commercial constuct experts to design a strategy to address key issues and challenges and pave the way for a seamless transtion to an offshore environment. 

We have done this successfully for clients in Payments and Cards issuing business, working with our clients to help get approvals from their clients to offshore. We will bring this experience to FIS to help enable and drive this faster.

Sutherland would propose to jointly review all such contracts where offshoring restrictions are present, to understand what considerations have gone into taking that view.

Once we jointly analyze, we can build a commong strategy to address the previous and ensuing concerns and build a joint "Go to Market" plan to take them to these clients.

We would leverage credible proof points, artefacts and other collaterals to impress upon them and make them agree to consider the offshoring option from operational, legal and regulatory compliance aspect.
GDPR — confirm DPA agreements, data processor obligations, cross-border transfer mechanisms, and breach notification procedures (including SLA to notify FIS).

Sutherland agrees to execute a mutually acceptable DPA covering all applicable data protection obligations across relevant privacy regulations globally. For cross-border transfers, Sutherland holds EU-US Data Privacy Framework certification including the UK and Swiss Extensions, is certified under both APEC CBPR and the Global CBPR Framework, and SCCs will be incorporated into the DPA. Sutherland maintains a robust breach management process and will strictly adhere to the agreed notification SLA to FIS as set out in the MSA/DPA.
US cardholder data — confirm CCPA compliance and your approach to state-level privacy law variation.

Sutherland's Privacy Information Management System (PIMS) is led by a designated Data Protection Officer (DPO) and incorporates the guidelines and controls required to meet applicable regulatory obligations. Sutherland’s compliance framework includes regular education and training, monitoring and auditing, and enforcement of compliance policies and procedures. The company’s privacy program includes administrative, physical, and electronic controls over confidential information, and specific policies regarding individuals’ rights to access, amend, and restrict the use of their personal information. Sutherland’s legal and compliance teams continuously monitor legislative and regulatory changes, including state-level privacy law variations, to ensure ongoing compliance and seamless implementation of necessary changes. Sutherland is also certified under ISO 27001 and ISO 27701, further demonstrating its commitment to robust data security and privacy management. These measures collectively ensure that Sutherland’s handling of US cardholder data is compliant with the CCPA and adaptable to state-level privacy law variations.
PCI DSS — confirm current certification level, scope, and most recent assessment date.

The current PCI DSS certification was attained on April 19, 2023, and applies to credit card lines of business. Sutherland has maintained PCI DSS compliance since 2010. The scope of certification is for credit card processing activities, supported by the implementation of Sentinel Secure, an innovative payment gateway ensuring 100 percent PCI compliance and agent fraud prevention. Details regarding the most recent assessment date are provided as April 19, 2023.
Regarding data ownership and portability, the context does not explicitly confirm that FIS retains full ownership and portability of all data at all times, nor does it describe the mechanism for full data extraction upon contract exit.
Confirm FIS retains full ownership and portability of all data at all times. Describe the mechanism for full data extraction upon contract exit.

Sutherland confirms that FIS always retains full ownership of all data. 
Sutherland acts solely as a data processor/service provider, with no rights, title, or interest in client data beyond what is required to deliver contracted services. These controls are governed under our ISO 27001 (ISMS), ISO 27701 (PIMS), PCI DSS, and SOC 2 compliant frameworks.
•	Data Ownership Assurance:
All data, including cardholder data (CHD), personal data, and associated metadata, is contractually recognized as the exclusive property of FIS. Sutherland enforces strict data handling, classification, and access controls to ensure data is processed only for authorized business purposes and in accordance with client instructions. 
•	Data Portability & Accessibility:
Sutherland ensures that client data is maintained in structured, commonly used, and machine-readable formats to support seamless portability. Data is logically segregated per client, enabling efficient extraction without commingling with other client datasets. 
•	Data Extraction Mechanism (Contract Exit):
Upon contract termination or upon request, Sutherland supports full data extraction through secure and controlled processes, including: 
o	Provision of data exports in agreed formats (e.g., CSV, encrypted databases, or other client-specified formats). 
o	Secure transfer mechanisms such as SFTP, encrypted storage media, or dedicated secure file exchange platforms. 
o	Validation and reconciliation of extracted data to ensure completeness and integrity. 
o	Defined timelines and SLAs to support timely data handover as per contractual agreements. 
•	Security During Data Transfer:
All data extraction and transfer activities are secured using strong encryption (in transit and at rest) in alignment with PCI DSS and industry best practices. Access to extraction processes is restricted, logged, and monitored to prevent unauthorized disclosure. 
•	Data Retention & Secure Disposal:
Following successful data handover and client confirmation, Sutherland performs secure data deletion in accordance with contractual requirements and regulatory standards. Certified data destruction methods are applied, and certificates of destruction are provided upon request. 
•	Audit & Compliance Assurance:
The entire process is governed by documented procedures and subject to internal and external audits under ISO 27001, ISO 27701, PCI DSS, and SOC 2 frameworks, ensuring transparency, accountability, and compliance. 
These measures ensure that FIS maintains continuous ownership and control of its data, with assured portability and secure, complete extraction upon contract exit.
Confirm logical and/or physical segregation of cardholder data across different FIS clients.

Sutherland confirms that robust logical and physical segregation controls are implemented to ensure strict isolation of cardholder data (CHD) . These controls are aligned with our ISO 27001, ISO 27701, and SOC 2 certified information security and privacy frameworks.
•	Logical Segregation:
Client environments are logically segregated through secure network architecture, including VLAN segmentation, firewalls, and access control mechanisms. Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) and least privilege principles are enforced to ensure that access to cardholder data is restricted to authorized personnel on a need-to-know basis. Dedicated application instances and database schemas further ensure tenant-level data isolation. 
•	Physical Segregation:
Where applicable, physical segregation is maintained through controlled access to data center facilities, restricted zones, and dedicated infrastructure for specific client requirements. Data centers are secured with multi-layered physical security controls, including biometric access, surveillance, and 24/7 monitoring. 
•	Data Protection Controls:
If stored in Sutherland environment Cardholder data is protected using strong encryption (both at rest and in transit), masking, and tokenization techniques where required. Data handling processes are governed by strict policies aligned with PCI DSS requirements.
Describe security controls for remote/WFH agents handling cardholder data (screen capture, device management, network security, print restrictions).

If the Card Data is stored in environment, Sutherland enforces stringent security controls for remote/WFH agents handling cardholder data (CHD), aligned with our ISO 27001, ISO 27701, PCI DSS, and SOC 2 compliance frameworks. These controls are designed to ensure confidentiality, integrity, and secure processing of sensitive payment information in remote environments.
•	Secure Access & Environment Controls:
WFH access for CHD processing is enabled only through controlled, risk-assessed environments and subject to client approval where required. Access is provisioned via secure Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI) or hardened remote access solutions with Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA). Sessions are fully monitored and restricted to prevent local data storage or data exfiltration. 
•	Screen Capture & Monitoring Controls:
Screen capture, recording, and real-time monitoring solutions are implemented in compliance with PCI DSS requirements. Controls include disabling unauthorized screen capture utilities, restricting copy/paste functions, and enabling session recording where contractually required. Data masking and pause-and-resume controls are enforced during sensitive data entry. 
•	Endpoint / Device Management:
Only Sutherland-managed and hardened endpoints are permitted for CHD access. Devices are configured with endpoint protection, host-based firewalls, disk encryption, and Data Loss Prevention (DLP) controls. Administrative privileges are restricted, and USB/external media access is disabled. Devices are continuously monitored and patched in line with defined security baselines. 
•	Network Security Controls:
Remote connectivity is secured through encrypted VPN tunnels or zero-trust network access (ZTNA) solutions with strong authentication. Network configurations enforce traffic inspection, intrusion detection/prevention, and geo/IP restrictions. Home network requirements are defined and validated, including secure Wi-Fi configurations (WPA2/WPA3) and prohibition of public or unsecured networks. 
•	Print & Data Handling Restrictions:
Printing of CHD is strictly prohibited in WFH environments. System-level controls prevent print screen, local printing, and data downloads. Clipboard restrictions, file transfer blocking, and watermarking mechanisms are enforced to prevent unauthorized data capture or sharing. 
•	Policy Enforcement & Awareness:
Agents are required to comply with strict clean desk/clear screen policies adapted for WFH, including workspace privacy requirements. Mandatory security and privacy training, including PCI DSS awareness, is completed prior to access provisioning and refreshed periodically. 
•	Monitoring, Logging & Compliance Assurance:
All remote sessions are logged and continuously monitored for anomalous activity. Security events are centrally managed via SIEM solutions, with defined incident response procedures. 
These layered controls ensure that cardholder data handled by remote/WFH agents remains secure, compliant, and protected against unauthorized access, disclosure, or misuse.
Who bears liability for regulatory fines resulting from vendor non-compliance? Describe indemnity and remediation framework for client disputes arising from vendor error.

Sutherland is contractually obligated to meet all mandated regulations and maintains robust compliance frameworks, including regular internal and external audits, to ensure adherence to regulatory requirements. In the event of disputes arising from vendor error, Sutherland has, on a very limited basis, indemnified vendors in the past. For example, Sutherland shared reasonable and nominal legal costs with a customer who was sued for alleged infringement related to a third-party software tool used by Sutherland in service delivery. 

Sutherland reserves the right to enter into further good faith contract negotiations regarding indemnity and other contractual provisions, based on the specifics of each deal. Significant corrective measures and consequence management are part of Sutherland’s policies and procedures in case of any non-adherence to regulatory compliance, supported by regular internal audits and compliance monitoring.
CFPB — describe your compliance program and oversight model for collections, disputes, and customer service operations.

Sutherland's collections, disputes and customer service operations are designed around US consumer lending laws—TCPA, FDCPA, Reg F, UDAAP, SCRA/MLA, FCRA, TILA, CFPB and state-specific rules.

Sutherland has developed and maintained a compliance management system (CMS) that is integrated into the overall management strategy of the organization. We take an integrated approach to our CMS that incorporates provisions from both our end and our clients to provide multiple benefits, not just at a strategic level but also at the operational, day-to-day level. 
The Sutherland Compliance Management System
•	Establishes its compliance responsibilities
•	Communicates those responsibilities to employees
•	Ensures business processes incorporate internal policies for meeting legal requirements
•	Reviews operations to ensure accountability for meeting assigned responsibilities and legal requirements
•	Takes corrective action and updates tools, systems, and materials as needed
A continuous CMS cycle allows Sutherland to create, maintain, and update the compliance management system so that it is effective in identifying, measuring, monitoring, controlling, and addressing compliance risks to consumers and to your organization.Sutherland Compliance Management System
•	Licensing, Reporting, Insurance, Taxing Authorities
•	Regulatory Review – Policy and Procedures
•	Compliance Training on Policy and Procedures
•	Internal Audit of the origination practices and LOS System 
•	Self-Testing and Reporting
•	External Audit - Regulatory Examination State and Federal, HUD
•	Complaint Management – Consumer, Regulatory, Employee, Client
•	Vendor Management – Lenders, Title, Suppliers, Warehouse Lines, BPO Clients etc. (DNC)
•	Quality Assurance and Quality Control
•	Legal Review – for risk mitigation 
Sutherland’s compliance policies and procedures ensure consistent operating guidelines that support the organization in complying with applicable federal consumer protection laws and regulations, both directly and through service providers. To further protect our clients each Sutherland employee engaged on a specific program as a dedicated employee are required to complete and adhere to the client’s compliance policies and procedures. The compliance policies and procedures provide employees with the information they need to perform their duties, protect the consumer and mitigate the client’s risks when engaging a third-party vendor. This may include applicable regulatory and legal citations and definitions, sample forms with instructions, and organization policy. Through industry monitoring, Sutherland will regularly review and update the compliance policies and procedures based upon new and revised federal and state consumer financial laws and regulations.
Sutherland actively operates under the following certifications and practices: PCI, ISO 27001:2000, P-CMM, Six Sigma, HIPAA, GDPR, EU-U.S. Privacy Shield Framework and COPC.  With over three decades of practitioner heritage, Sutherland has evolved to build its own Service Excellence Framework based on internal and external benchmarking of proven best practices.

In relation to our Banking and Financial Services operations, we are currently progressing through our FCA compliance programme and are committed to achieving full compliance within a defined timeframe.
